004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
029
030/**
031 * A streaming HTTP request body that emits its content through a token/record-streaming cursor.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * Lets a caller pass a {@code Consumer<RecordWriter>} or {@code Consumer<TokenWriter>} as the body of a request.
035 * The consumer is invoked lazily during {@link #writeTo(OutputStream)} &mdash; the cursor writes directly to the
036 * live transport output stream, so arbitrarily large payloads are streamed to the wire without being buffered in
037 * memory first.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * While the next-generation client negotiates a parser for inbound/response bodies (from the response
041 * {@code Content-Type}), the request-body OUTPUT format here is an explicit caller choice: the {@link Serializer}
042 * that backs the cursor is supplied directly (defaulting to {@link JsonSerializer#DEFAULT}).  The body's
043 * {@link #getContentType()} reflects that serializer's media type.
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Repeatability:</h5>
046 * <p>
047 * Streaming bodies are <b>non-repeatable</b> by default ({@link #isRepeatable()} returns {@code false}) because the
048 * caller's consumer may be backed by a one-shot source.  A non-repeatable body fails fast (throws {@link IOException})
049 * if a resend is required (i.e. {@link #writeTo(OutputStream)} is invoked more than once).  Callers whose consumer can
050 * be replayed safely may opt in via {@link #repeatable()}.
051 *
052 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
053 * <p class='bjava'>
054 *    <ja>@Remote</ja>
055 *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyApi {
056 *
057 *       <ja>@RemotePost</ja>(<js>"/bulk-upload"</js>)
058 *       <jk>void</jk> upload(<ja>@Content</ja> RecordStreamBody <jv>body</jv>);
059 *    }
060 *
061 *    <jc>// Caller</jc>
062 *    <jv>api</jv>.upload(RecordStreamBody.<jsm>records</jsm>(<jv>w</jv> -&gt; {
063 *       <jk>for</jk> (Bean <jv>b</jv> : <jv>source</jv>())
064 *          <jv>w</jv>.write(<jv>b</jv>);
065 *    }));
066 * </p>
067 *
068 * <p>
069 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
070 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
071 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
072 * (and possibly earlier).
073 *
074 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
075 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
076 * </ul>
077 *
078 * @since 9.2.1
079 */
080public final class RecordStreamBody implements HttpBody {
081
082   /**
083    * Creates a body that streams via a {@link RecordWriter} (whole-value record cursor) using the default JSON serializer.
084    *
085    * @param consumer The callback that emits records. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
086    * @return A new body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
087    */
088   public static RecordStreamBody records(Consumer<RecordWriter> consumer) {
089      return records(JsonSerializer.DEFAULT, consumer);
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * Creates a body that streams via a {@link RecordWriter} (whole-value record cursor) using the given serializer.
094    *
095    * @param serializer The serializer that opens the record cursor. Must not be <jk>null</jk> and must implement
096    *    {@link RecordWritable}.
097    * @param consumer The callback that emits records. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
098    * @return A new body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
099    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the serializer does not support the record-writer surface.
100    */
101   public static RecordStreamBody records(Serializer serializer, Consumer<RecordWriter> consumer) {
102      assertArgNotNull("serializer", serializer);
103      assertArgNotNull("consumer", consumer);
104      if (! (serializer instanceof RecordWritable))
105         throw iaex("Serializer ''{0}'' does not support the record-writer surface.", serializer.getClass().getName());
106      return new RecordStreamBody(serializer, consumer, RecordWriter.class, false);
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Creates a body that streams via a {@link TokenWriter} (fine-grained structural cursor) using the default JSON serializer.
111    *
112    * @param consumer The callback that emits structural events. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
113    * @return A new body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
114    */
115   public static RecordStreamBody token(Consumer<TokenWriter> consumer) {
116      return token(JsonSerializer.DEFAULT, consumer);
117   }
118
119   /**
120    * Creates a body that streams via a {@link TokenWriter} (fine-grained structural cursor) using the given serializer.
121    *
122    * @param serializer The serializer that opens the token cursor. Must not be <jk>null</jk> and must implement
123    *    {@link TokenWritable}.
124    * @param consumer The callback that emits structural events. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
125    * @return A new body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
126    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the serializer does not support the token-writer surface.
127    */
128   public static RecordStreamBody token(Serializer serializer, Consumer<TokenWriter> consumer) {
129      assertArgNotNull("serializer", serializer);
130      assertArgNotNull("consumer", consumer);
131      if (! (serializer instanceof TokenWritable))
132         throw iaex("Serializer ''{0}'' does not support the token-writer surface.", serializer.getClass().getName());
133      return new RecordStreamBody(serializer, consumer, TokenWriter.class, false);
134   }
135
136   private final Serializer serializer;
137   private final Consumer<?> consumer;
138   private final Class<?> writerKind;
139   private final boolean repeatable;
140   private boolean written;
141
142   private RecordStreamBody(Serializer serializer, Consumer<?> consumer, Class<?> writerKind, boolean repeatable) {
143      this.serializer = serializer;
144      this.consumer = consumer;
145      this.writerKind = writerKind;
146      this.repeatable = repeatable;
147   }
148
149   /**
150    * Returns a copy of this body flagged as repeatable.
151    *
152    * <p>
153    * Only opt in when the caller's consumer can be safely replayed (e.g. it iterates a re-readable source).
154    *
155    * @return A new repeatable body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
156    */
157   public RecordStreamBody repeatable() {
158      return new RecordStreamBody(serializer, consumer, writerKind, true);
159   }
160
161   @Override /* HttpBody */
162   public String getContentType() {
163      var mt = serializer.getResponseContentType();
164      return mt == null ? null : mt.toString();
165   }
166
167   @Override /* HttpBody */
168   public long getContentLength() {
169      return -1;
170   }
171
172   @Override /* HttpBody */
173   public boolean isRepeatable() {
174      return repeatable;
175   }
176
177   @Override /* HttpBody */
178   @SuppressWarnings({
179      "unchecked", // Consumer is paired with writerKind at construction; the cast matches the opened cursor type.
180      "resource"   // The cursor wraps the caller-owned 'out'; its close() flushes but does not close 'out'.
181   })
182   public void writeTo(OutputStream out) throws IOException {
183      assertArgNotNull("out", out);
184      if (written && ! repeatable)
185         throw new IOException("Non-repeatable streaming body cannot be resent.  Mark the body repeatable() if its producer can be replayed.");
186      written = true;
187      try (var w = (writerKind == TokenWriter.class)
188            ? ((TokenWritable) serializer).serializeTokens(out)
189            : ((RecordWritable) serializer).serializeRecords(out)) {
190         ((Consumer<RecordWriter>) consumer).accept(w);
191         w.flush();
192      }
193   }
194}
